synsets.definition: a state of acute pain
synsets.usage_example: His suffering was written across his face, each breath sharp and labored as he endured the acute pain.

synsets.definition: make angry
synsets.usage_example: The insult made him angry, his face flushing as heat rose in his chest and his fists clenched.

synsets.definition: a vague unpleasant emotion that is experienced in anticipation of some (usually ill-defined) misfortune
synsets.usage_example: Her anxiety about the upcoming surgery manifested as sleepless nights and a tight knot in her stomach as she anticipated the unknown.

synsets.definition: the trait of lacking enthusiasm for or interest in things generally
synsets.usage_example: She regarded the news with complete indifference, neither celebrating nor mourning, her expression remaining blank.

synsets.definition: an overwhelming feeling of wonder or admiration

properties.relation: awe radiates with light and brilliance
awe moves to action or creation

synsets.usage_example: Standing before the vast cathedral, she felt awe wash over her at the towering beauty and boundless grandeur of the architecture.

synsets.definition: the property of having a harsh unpleasant taste

properties.relation: bitter taste feels harsh on the tongue
bitter has a sharp, cutting sensation
bitter taste lingers long after swallowing
bitter coats the back of the mouth
bitter tastes are often acquired over time
bitter remarks are caustic or acerbic

synsets.usage_example: The coffee was too bitter to drink without sugar.

synsets.definition: a state of extreme happiness

properties.relation: in bliss, the mind becomes still
bliss suspends the sense of time

synsets.usage_example: In that moment of perfect understanding, she experienced complete bliss.

synsets.definition: the humane quality of understanding the suffering of others and wanting to do something about it

properties.relation: compassion is receptive to others' suffering
compassion is inclusive of all beings

synsets.usage_example: Her compassion for the homeless man moved her to offer him shelter and food.

synsets.definition: happiness with one's situation in life

properties.relation: contentment has a warm, settled feeling
contentment anchors one to the present
contentment makes less seem like enough

synsets.usage_example: After years of striving, she finally found contentment in her quiet life.

synsets.definition: a quality of spirit that enables you to face danger or pain without showing fear

properties.relation: courage is steadfast in the face of danger
courage burns like an inner fire
courage is fearlessness in the face of danger
courage can be defiant in adversity

synsets.usage_example: The firefighter showed great courage entering the burning building to rescue the child.

synsets.definition: a feeling of extreme pleasure or satisfaction
synsets.usage_example: She laughed with delight when she opened her birthday gift.

synsets.definition: the feeling that everything is wrong and nothing will turn out well
synsets.usage_example: After months of failed attempts, he sank into despair and stopped trying.

synsets.definition: strong feelings of dislike
synsets.usage_example: The rotting smell filled her with disgust and she turned away.

synsets.definition: be afraid or scared of; be frightened of
synsets.usage_example: She felt fear grip her chest as the car skidded on the icy road.

synsets.definition: street names for methylenedioxymethamphetamine

properties.relation: heightens perception of light and brightness
heightens sensitivity to touch and texture
associated with group and club settings

synsets.usage_example: The substance known as 'go' on the street produces a distinctive combination of euphoria and physical stimulation.

synsets.definition: a feeling of joy and pride
synsets.usage_example: After receiving praise for her work, she felt a lightness in her chest and couldn't help smiling.

synsets.definition: feel envious towards; admire enviously

properties.relation: obsessive focus on object of envy

synsets.usage_example: She envied her friend's effortless success, a feeling that gnawed at her constantly.

synsets.definition: a feeling of great (usually exaggerated) elation
synsets.usage_example: After the announcement, the team experienced euphoria, their energy seemingly boundless.

synsets.definition: be sorry; used to introduce an unpleasant statement
synsets.usage_example: As the storm approached, she felt fear rising in her chest, making every breath shallow.

synsets.definition: the feeling that accompanies an experience of being thwarted in attaining your goals
synsets.usage_example: Frustration mounted as each attempt to fix the broken device met with failure.

synsets.definition: a feeling of intense anger

properties.relation: impulse to harm or break things

synsets.usage_example: She felt madness rising in her chest as she listened to his lies.

synsets.definition: a feeling of melancholy apprehension

properties.relation: thick, unclear, hard to see through

synsets.usage_example: A sense of gloom settled over the city as news of the disaster spread.

synsets.definition: a feeling of thankfulness and appreciation
synsets.usage_example: She felt deep gratitude for her friend's kindness during her illness.

synsets.definition: something that causes great unhappiness

properties.relation: weighs heavily on body and soul
brings absence of light and hope

synsets.usage_example: The loss of her beloved grandmother was a grief she would carry for years.

synsets.definition: expect and wish
synsets.usage_example: She held onto hope that things would improve.

synsets.definition: an instance in which you are caused to lose your prestige or self-respect

properties.relation: feeling of being exposed and vulnerable
often occurs in front of witnesses
unable to undo or escape the moment
creates sense of separation from others

synsets.usage_example: She felt humiliation wash over her when her mistake was announced to the entire room.

synsets.definition: a feeling of righteous anger

properties.relation: anger is experienced as intense heat
has intensity and brightness of fire
demands to be voiced and expressed

synsets.usage_example: His outrage at the injustice drove him to speak out publicly.

synsets.definition: (pathology) abnormal sensitivity to stimulation
synsets.usage_example: The wound showed signs of irritation where the bandage had rubbed repeatedly.

synsets.definition: zealous vigilance

properties.relation: muscles and nerves on high alert
concentrated attention on what is valued
ready to defend against perceived threat
underlying worry of loss or betrayal
bars others from access to valued thing

synsets.usage_example: The dragon's jealousy over his treasure hoard was legendary; he allowed no one near it.

synsets.definition: something or someone that provides a source of happiness
synsets.usage_example: Her newborn daughter was a source of pure joy, bringing delight to the entire family.

synsets.definition: prolonged unfulfilled desire or need

properties.relation: longing reaches toward what is absent

synsets.usage_example: She felt a deep longing for her childhood home.

synsets.definition: a strong positive emotion of regard and affection
synsets.usage_example: Their love for each other was evident in every gesture.

synsets.definition: characterized by or causing or expressing sadness
synsets.usage_example: A melancholy mood settled over her as she watched the rain fall.

synsets.definition: longing for something past

properties.relation: nostalgia pulls you toward the past

synsets.usage_example: He felt a wave of nostalgia looking through old photographs.

synsets.definition: partial or total lack of sensation in a part of the body; a symptom of nerve damage or dysfunction

properties.relation: numbness is an absence of sensation

synsets.usage_example: The injury resulted in partial numbness below the waist.

synsets.definition: sudden mass fear and anxiety over anticipated events

properties.relation: panic causes heartbeat and thoughts to race
flush of adrenaline causes heat and sweating
panic triggers automatic responses without thought
panic clouds judgment and spatial awareness
extreme fear can freeze movement or trigger flight
panic floods the mind with terror
fear obscures rational thought and perception

synsets.usage_example: When the fire alarm sounded, panic seized the crowd and they rushed toward the exits.

synsets.definition: any object of warm affection or devotion

properties.relation: object of affection radiates emotional warmth
beloved person or thing is delicate, requiring gentle care
beloved is held near in physical or emotional space
loved objects are often perceived as aesthetically pleasing
beloved seems to glow in the lover's perception
loved object is held dear and treasured
inspires loyalty and dedication in the lover
cannot be substituted or recovered if lost
loved object elevates and improves the lover
provides stability and anchor for the lover

synsets.usage_example: She held her greatest love close, treasuring every moment with her daughter.

synsets.definition: an unfortunate development

properties.relation: misfortune is associated with darkness and gloom
burden of bad fortune weighs down
misfortune brings emotional coldness and isolation
by definition, unfortunate developments are undesired
often perceived as unjust or undeserved
misfortune can separate individuals from community

synsets.usage_example: The company's bankruptcy was a great shame that affected thousands of employees.

synsets.definition: behave violently, as if in state of a great anger

properties.relation: storming is characterized by forceful, aggressive action
exerts intense physical force and impact
anger generates intense heat and intensity
angry storming is quick and accelerated
storming can damage relationships and surroundings
storming may provide temporary emotional release

synsets.usage_example: He stormed out of the meeting, slamming the door behind him.

synsets.definition: a state of elated bliss

properties.relation: joy bubbles up and fizzes with energy
person glows and shines with joy
ecstasy crackles with energy and vitality
state of extreme happiness and elation
ecstasy is at the peak of human experience
ecstatic state loses sense of temporal flow
joy floods all thought and sensation
joy opens up and spreads outward

synsets.usage_example: She was in ecstasy when she learned she had won the competition.

synsets.definition: a feeling of deep regret (usually for some misdeed)
synsets.usage_example: After betraying his best friend's confidence, he was filled with remorse.

synsets.definition: a feeling of deep and bitter anger and ill-will
synsets.usage_example: Years of unfair treatment filled her with bitterness toward the organization.

synsets.definition: a disposition free from stress or emotion
synsets.usage_example: In his quiet way, he reflected on the day's events without anxiety.

synsets.definition: a painful emotion resulting from an awareness of inadequacy or guilt
synsets.usage_example: When she realized her error had hurt innocent people, shame overwhelmed her.

synsets.definition: an emotion of great sadness associated with loss or bereavement
synsets.usage_example: The depth of her sorrow after her mother's passing lasted for years.

synsets.definition: hurt the feelings of

properties.relation: emotional pain feels sharp and cutting

synsets.usage_example: Her dismissive comment hurt him deeply, leaving him feeling rejected and ashamed.

synsets.definition: a tendency to express warm and affectionate feeling

properties.relation: allows time and space for others

synsets.usage_example: She touched his shoulder with tenderness, and he felt her genuine affection.

synsets.definition: a feeling of intense annoyance caused by being tormented

properties.relation: same annoying pattern again and again

synsets.usage_example: The constant harassment from the office left her exhausted and frayed.

synsets.definition: the state of being vulnerable or exposed
synsets.usage_example: His vulnerability showed in the tremor of his voice as he confessed his fears.

synsets.definition: belligerence aroused by a real or supposed wrong (personified as one of the deadly sins)
synsets.usage_example: His anger flared when he discovered the betrayal, burning through his composure.

synsets.definition: prolonged unfulfilled desire or need

properties.relation: longing creates an ache in the chest
longing reveals what one truly values

synsets.usage_example: She felt a deep longing for her childhood home, an ache that never quite faded with time.
